Title: Expression of the Arabidopsis WRINKLED 1 transcription factor leads to higher accumulation of palmitate in soybean seed

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-ACP chain termination step affecting saturated/monounsaturated C18 fatty acid output
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes stearoyl-ACP to release free stearic acid from ACP-bound fatty acyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Stearic acid; oleic acid-enriched soybean oil with increased total saturates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms stearate pool and shifts acyl flux composition in seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In transgenic soybean, increases stearate strongly; when combined with FAD2-1 silencing increases oleate and total saturates, and when stacked with AtWRI1 contributes to high-solid soybean oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in soybean; genetic stacking by crossing; single-cassette expression; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-FID/GC
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Soybean β-conglycinin promoter, seed-specific; phaseolin promoter, seed-specific
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in developing soybean seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mangosteen GmFatA1 expression in soybean increased seed stearate to ~17–19%. 2. Combining GmFatA1 with FAD2-1 silencing raised stearate and oleate while lowering palmitate. 3. In triple stacks with AtWRI1, GmFatA1 contributed to oils with elevated saturates and oleate and improved functionality.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher saturates alter melting behavior; in some stacks palmitate decreased substantially; no clear total oil gain reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering high-stearate, high-solid soybean oil; can be stacked with FAD2 suppression and AtWRI1 to tailor bakery/frying oil profiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text calls this gene GmFatA1 although source species is Garcinia mangostana; previous work established the allele used in soybean.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal fatty acid desaturase FAD2 family
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch step controlling oleate versus polyunsaturate partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oleic acid to linoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by consuming oleate to form linoleate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GmFAD2-1 greatly increases oleate and reduces polyunsaturated fatty acids; with GmFatA1 increases stearate and oleate but lowers palmitate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi/silencing transgene in soybean; gene stacking; single-cassette silencing-expression design; seed fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Common bean phaseolin promoter, seed-specific; phaseolin promoter in pPTN1289/pPTN1301, seed-specific
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmFAD2-1 silencing raised oleate to ~60–74% and sharply reduced linoleate/linolenate. 2. Combined with GmFatA1, it produced high-stearate, high-oleate oil with low palmitate. 3. In triple stacks with AtWRI1, oleate remained high while palmitate was partly restored.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced polyunsaturates and altered seed oil composition may affect physical properties; in combinations palmitate can fall below wild type
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core engineering target for high-oleic soybean oil; effective when stacked with thioesterases and regulators to optimize oxidative stability and solid fat content
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as a silencing target rather than overexpressed enzyme; event 374-1 and constructs pPTN1289/pPTN1301 carry FAD2-1 silencing elements.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes palmitoyl-ACP to release free palmitic acid from plastid fatty acid synthesis intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous GmFatB2a is specifically upregulated by AtWRI1 in soybean seeds and is identified as the underlying mechanism for increased palmitate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot of FatB signal; allele-specific RT-PCR and qRT-PCR for four GmFatB alleles; metabolite profiling in AtWRI1 transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter in soybean; effect elicited by seed-specific AtWRI1 transgene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in immature embryos/seeds of AtWRI1-expressing soybean; wild-type background primarily expressed in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among four soybean FatB alleles, only GmFatB2a was strongly induced in AtWRI1-containing events. 2. Its transcript increased by about 30-fold in pPTN1301 event 1026-1 versus wild type. 3. This induction explains the elevated palmitate phenotype caused by AtWRI1 in soybean seed.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage: immature embryos; field and greenhouse phenotypes assessed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher palmitate contributes to higher saturates and may affect low-temperature germination/stand establishment indirectly through seed oil composition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous target for increasing palmitate in soybean seed without directly introducing exotic thioesterase alleles; useful for trait tuning with high-oleic backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in flowers and leaves in wild-type soybean background
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of palmitate output in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AW-box-like motifs were found in exon 1 rather than upstream promoter region; direct binding was not experimentally proven.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to solid fat content and oil functionality for baking/industrial applications; saturated/oleic-rich oils have improved oxidative stability
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis and carbon partitioning during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmFatB2a; ACC/BCCP2; other lipid-biosynthesis genes monitored including PK, ACP1, KASI, PDH1a
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates endogenous soybean FatB2a strongly and increases ACC expression; modulates lipid biosynthetic gene expression in developing seeds
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator causing the high-palmitate phenotype in soybean seeds; specifically restores palmitate in otherwise low-palmitate high-stearate/high-oleate stacks
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific expression of AtWRI1 in soybean increases palmitate to about 18–20% with little or no increase in total oil; in stacks it restores palmitate while maintaining elevated stearate/oleate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in soybean; field and greenhouse seed fatty acid profiling; northern blot; RT-PCR/qRT-PCR of downstream genes; stacking analyses
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in immature embryos/developing seeds under a seed-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed-specific β-conglycinin promoter-driven transgene expression during embryogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field versus greenhouse growth; low-temperature germination assays for altered oil phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No consistent increase in total oil; associated oil composition may reduce germination performance at 10°C and may require identity preservation for deployment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for raising palmitate and total saturates in seed oil; can be stacked with GmFatA1 and FAD2-1 silencing to create high-solid, high-oleic soybean oils for baking and industrial use
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRI1 expression in soybean increased seed palmitate by 36–69% above controls, often reaching ~18–20%. 2. The increased palmitate is linked to specific upregulation of endogenous GmFatB2a, not other FatB alleles. 3. AtWRI1 combined with GmFatA1 and FAD2-1 silencing produced oils with elevated saturates and high oleate, improving oxidative stability and functionality.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean-codon-optimized AtWRI1 ORF was used; direct binding to GmFatB2a promoter/exonic AW-box was inferred but not demonstrated experimentally.
